title: Abrahamia grandidieri
taxonomy: Abrahamia grandidieri is classified within the family Anacardiaceae and belongs to the genus Abrahamia. This plant is native to Madagascar and is a part of the Sapindales order in the broader classification. The species name grandidieri is named after the French naturalist and explorer Joseph Grandidier. Abrahamia grandidieri is closely related to other species in the same genus, such as Abrahamia madagascariensis. Within the family Anacardiaceae, Abrahamia grandidieri is further classified as part of the subfamily Anacardioideae.

The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects.
